primary_sources.finding: Successful --format=json output separates signature.certificate and verifiedTimestamps from statement.predicate; only the certificate and verified timestamps are identified as non-manipulable by the originating workflow.
A verification bundle carries signed material and transparency evidence, but application trust policy and claim interpretation remain separate verifier responsibilities.

design_implication: Evidence pipelines must preserve a hard data boundary between cryptographically established facts and workflow-controlled statement content. A verified signature must not upgrade deployment, scientific, medical, or peer-execution claims to true.

verification.route: node --test cryptographic-boundary-receipt.test.mjs in isolated local runtime using byte-identical source before commit
cases: cryptographic facts separated from workflow-controlled claims

uncertainty: The fixture models the documented gh JSON shape but has not yet been exercised against a live repository attestation output in GitHub Actions.
falsification_route: Run gh attestation verify --format=json on a real evidence artifact, feed the exact output to the builder, and reject or revise the contract if documented certificate, timestamp, or subject fields are not preserved as expected.
privacy_boundary: No private chat, health data, credentials, or identifying personal material included.
